title: Existence and uniqueness of fractal-fractional equations generated by a new fractal-fractional operator utilizing the advanced gamma function
author: Alazman, Ibrahim
description: Numerous special functions, including the beta function, hypergeometric functions, and other orthogonal polynomials, are closely connected to the gamma function. Recently, gamma function has been enhanced by adding a new parameter. As a consequence, this gamma is called the parametric gamma function or b-gamma function. By utilizing the enhanced gamma function (or the parametric gamma function), we have present a generalization for the special function Rabotnov function. Consequently, new fractal-fractional operators (derivative and integral) involving the generalized Rabotnov function are defined. Analysis is introduced to discover the main properties of the suggested operators. Since the main challenge in the calculus of fractal-fractional operators is to design examples, we illustrate a set of examples including power series. We explore the boundedness of the recommended operators. There are many gains for checking the boundedness of fractal operators in general and fractal-fractional operators in particular. Moreover, as an application, we establish the existence and uniqueness solution of abstract fractal-fractional equation. Examples are presented at the end of the effort. Graphics and computations are observed using MATHEMATICA 13.3 software.вҖўBy using the parametric gamma function, the Rabotnov special function is generalized. New fractal-fractional operators are presented using the generalized Rabotnov function with examples;вҖўBoundedness of these operators is investigated, where bounded operators give a strong foundation for understanding linear transformations across normed spaces, with many applications in science and mathematics.вҖўConditions of the existence and uniqueness of solutions of fractal-fractional differential abstract equation are established.

steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: The particular circumstances of the equations and the corresponding theorems determine the existence and uniqueness of solutions to differential equations. The standards for determining these attributes may vary throughout differential equation categories. In order to analyze and solve differential equations, the use of theorems such as the Picard-Lindelof theorem and uniqueness theorem is essential. In this section, we consider the following abstract fractal-fractional differential equation(1)bCО”tОә,ОҪП…(t)=V(t,П…(t));or(2)bRLО”tОә,ОҪП…(t)=V(t,П…(t)),where П…(0)=П…0 and V:[0,в„ҳ]Г—RвҶ’R is continuous and differentiable. Consider the Banach space B of all continuous functions over the interval J=[0,в„ҳ],в„ҳ<вҲһ subjecting to the sup norm.

steps_raw.instruction: Next result shows the existence of nonzero solution for Eq. (1).

(1) admits a solution П…. In addition, if V is a Lipschitz with respect to the second variableвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘвүӨlвҲҘП…(t)вҲ’ПҲ(t)вҲҘ,such thatlвҢ©1ОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә,ОҪвҢӘ0then the solution is unique.

steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: By taking the supremum of the above inequality, we have QвҲҲS (boundedness of Q). We proceed to show that Q is continuous in S. Since V is continuous in J then it is uniformly continuous satisfyingвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘвүӨПөОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә.

steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: A computation brings|Q(П…)(t)вҲ’Q(ПҲ)(t)|вүӨОҪвҲ«0t|V(П„,П…(П„))вҲ’V(П„,ПҲ(П„))||[О Оә]b(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)Оәb|dП„вүӨОҪвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘвҲ«0t|((tвҲ’П„)b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Оһ(1+Оә/bb,1+Оә/bb)(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)(1+Оә/b)/bb(1+Оә/b)/b)|dП„вүӨОҪвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Г—вҲ«0t|(tвҲ’П„))(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Оһ(1+Оә/bb,1+Оә/bb)(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)(1+Оә/b)/bb(1+Оә/b)/b)|dП„вүӨвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1вҲ«0t|(tвҲ’П„))(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1|dП„вүӨОөОҪОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә=Оө.Let t1,t2вҲҲJ such that t1вү t2. Then|Q(П…)(t1)вҲ’Q(П…)(t2)|вүӨ2|П…0|+ОҪвҲ«0t1|V(П„,П…(П„))||[О Оә]b(вҲ’О»(t1вҲ’П„)Оәb|dП„+ОҪвҲ«0t2|V(П„,П…(П„))||[О Оә]b(вҲ’О»(t2вҲ’П„)Оәb|dП„вүӨ2|П…0|+LОҪвҲҘП…вҲҘвҲ«0t1|((t1вҲ’П„)b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Оһ(1+Оә/bb,1+Оә/bb)(вҲ’О»(t1вҲ’П„)(1+Оә/b)/bb(1+Оә/b)/b)|dП„+LОҪвҲҘП…вҲҘвҲ«0t2|((t2вҲ’П„)b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Оһ(1+Оә/bb,1+Оә/bb)(вҲ’О»(t2вҲ’П„)(1+Оә/b)/bb(1+Оә/b)/b)|dП„вүӨ2|П…0|+ОҪLвҲҘП…вҲҘ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1(вҲ«0t1|(t1вҲ’П„))(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1|dП„+вҲ«0t2|(t2вҲ’П„))(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1|dП„)вүӨ2|П…0|+2ОҪLвҲҘП…вҲҘ(1b)Оәb+1bвҲ’1(b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә)Thus, we obtain|Q(П…)(t1)вҲ’Q(П…)(t2)|вүӨ2|П…0|1вҲ’LОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1(b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә),П…0вү 0.Thus, Q is eqicontinuous in S. As a consequence, Arzela-Ascoli theorem yields that Q(S) is relatively compact operator. The Schauder fixed point theorem possesses a fixed point which is corresponding to the solution of (1).

steps_raw.instruction: For the uniqueness, we have to employ the Banach fixed point theorem.|Q(П…)(t)вҲ’Q(ПҲ)(t)|вүӨОҪвҲ«0t|V(П„,П…(П„))вҲ’V(П„,ПҲ(П„))||[О Оә]b(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)Оәb|dП„вүӨвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘОҪвҲ«0t|((tвҲ’П„)b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Оһ(1+Оә/bb,1+Оә/bb)(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)(1+Оә/b)/bb(1+Оә/b)/b)|dП„вүӨвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Г—вҲ«0t|(tвҲ’П„))(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1Оһ(1+Оә/bb,1+Оә/bb)(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)(1+Оә/b)/bb(1+Оә/b)/b)|dП„вүӨвҲҘV(t,П…(t))вҲ’V(t,ПҲ(t))вҲҘОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1вҲ«0t|(tвҲ’П„))(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1|dП„вүӨ(в„“ОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә)вҲҘП…вҲ’ПҲвҲҘ,where(lОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә)<1.Hence, the operator Q is a contraction mapping, then the Banach fixed point theorem shows that Q has a single fixed point that relates to the outcome of Eq. (1).

The solution takes the formulaП…(t)=П…0+ОҪвҲ«0tV(П„,П…(П„))[О Оә]b(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)ОәbdП„=12+12вҲ«0tП…(П„)8[О 12]2(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)14dП„.When b=3, we getLОҪ(1b)(Оә/b+1)/bвҲ’1(b2в„ҳb+Оәb2b+Оә)=0.314505 l=0.125.Hence, it admits a unique solution. The solution takes the formulaП…(t)=П…0+вҲ«0tV(П„,П…(П„))[О Оә]b(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)ОәbdП„=12+12вҲ«0tП…(П„)8[О 12]3(вҲ’О»(tвҲ’П„)16dП„.

steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: Remark 1 Example 2 is a special type of Fractal-fractional Riccati equation. In several distinct domains, including differential geometry, mathematical physics, and control theory, the Riccati equation exists. It could sometimes be feasible to find closed-form solutions to the general Riccati problem, given its seemingly straightforward nature. The Riccati equation is commonly used in control theory to analyze optimum control issues, where finding the solution is crucial to identifying the best control tactics. It occurs in nonlinear differential equation problems in mathematical physics, and its solutions are frequently important for comprehending the movements of physical systems.
